zh.Broseley, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾布洛瑟利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Langley於1325年出生在英國藍卡爾鮑頓時，他的父親John de Langley I25歲，母親Mrs John de Langley14歲。他於1350年與Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow在英國藍卡爾鮑頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1369年在英國藍卡爾米德萊頓享年44歲。

zh-hans.Broseley, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾布洛瑟利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Langley于1325年出生在英国藍卡爾鮑頓时，他的父亲John de Langley I25岁，母亲Mrs John de Langley14岁。他于1350年与Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow在英国藍卡爾鮑頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1369年在英国藍卡爾米德萊頓享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Langley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Langley or Longley (in Kent, Sussex, Hertfordshire, Norfolk, Somerset, Shrops, Lancashire, Durham, and many other counties), themselves from Old English lang ‘long’ + lēah ‘wood, glade, clearing’.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Langlif (Old Norse Langlíf ‘long’ + ‘life’ or ‘body’).

variants.name: Langlais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Marcel, Normand, Albertine, Armand, Cecile, Enoil, Herve, Jacques, Laurent.
French: ethnic name for an Englishman, from Anglais ‘Englishman’, with fused definite article l'. Compare Langley .

History: Daniel Sargent dit Sérien and Langlais from Worcester, MA married Marguerite Lavoie in Rivière-Ouelle, QC, in 1718. Joseph Weber dit Langlais from Boston, MA married Marie-Charlotte Guillet in Cap Saint-Ignace, QC, in 1725.

variants.name: Longley
meanings: English (mainly Nottinghamshire and South Yorkshire): variant of   Langley  . Compare   Longpre   2.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Langley was born in 1325, in Broughton, Lancashire, England, his father, John de Langley I, was 25 and his mother, Mrs John de Langley, was 14. He married Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow in 1350, in Broughton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1369, in Middleton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Langley est né en 1325, à Broughton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, John de Langley I, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs John de Langley, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow en 1350, à Broughton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1369, à Middleton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Langley 1325 in Broughton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Langley I 25 und seine Mutter Mrs John de Langley 14. Er hat 1350 in Broughton, Lancashire, England Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1369 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Middleton, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Langley è nato il nel 1325 nel Broughton, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, John de Langley I, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs John de Langley, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow nel 1350, nel Broughton, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1369, nel Middleton, Lancashire, England, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Langleyが1325年にイギリスランカシュアイアブロウトンで生まれた時，父John de Langley Iは25歳，母Mrs John de Langleyは14歳であった。1350年にイギリスランカシュアイアブロウトンでJoan   "Joanna" de Tetlowと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1369年にイギリスランカシュアイアミッドレトンで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Langley(이)가 1325년 영국 랭카실르 브루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Langley I(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs John de Langley(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1350년 영국 랭카실르 브루그h턴에서 Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1369년 영국 랭카실르 미들레턴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Langley nasceu em 1325, em Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Langley I, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs John de Langley, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow em 1350, em Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1369, em Middleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Langley родился в 1325 г. в местности броугтон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Langley I было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs John de Langley, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow в 1350 г. в местности броугтон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1369 г. в местности миддлетон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Langley nació en 1325, en Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Langley I, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs John de Langley, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Joan   "Joanna" de Tetlow en 1350, en Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1369, en Middleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
